Title: Gibberellin signaling regulates pectin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ga3ox1-3 reduces mucilage and pectin-related gene expression; exogenous GA3 restores defect
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Reduced endogenous GA biosynthesis in ga3ox1-3 lowers mucilage pectin accumulation. 2. GA3 application fully rescues the ga3ox1-3 mucilage defect. 3. GA biosynthesis level therefore positively influences pectin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ga3ox1-3 shows reduced mucilage and growth defects that are GA-rescuable
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating GA biosynthesis can modulate pectin traits but likely affects broad GA-dependent growth processes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a common upstream hormone-metabolism gene rather than a direct pectin-pathway regulator

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ent-copalyl diphosphate synthase; GA biosynthesis enzyme
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ga1-3 reduces pectin/mucilage and seedling growth; defects are rescued by GA3 and by DELLA loss or partially by MYB5 overexpression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; GA3 rescue; genetic interaction with rga-2 and dellaD; MYB5 overexpression rescue
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Severe GA deficiency in ga1-3 reduces seed mucilage, seedling pectin, and growth. 2. Exogenous GA3 restores these defects. 3. DELLA mutations and MYB5 overexpression genetically suppress ga1-3 phenotypes, linking GA biosynthesis to the DELLA-MBW-TTG2 pectin module.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong growth defects and infertility without GA supplementation
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for MYB5 overexpression in ga1-3 background
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Demonstrates upstream hormonal leverage over pectin accumulation, but GA deficiency causes severe pleiotropy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used primarily as hormonal-genetic entry point to establish GA dependence of pectin biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Pectin (seed mucilage pectin; RG-I-enriched; seedling pectin)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell adhesion; wall integrity; contributes to seedling growth
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: RG-I biosynthesis regulatory module for seed mucilage; broader seedling pectin module
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Developing siliques/seed coat; also functional in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pectin biosynthesis; reduced GL2 function decreases pectin/GalA content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (gl2-8); ChIP-qPCR of upstream regulators to GL2 promoter; dual-LUC reporter assays; RT-qPCR of downstream genes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GL2 is the direct downstream target of the GA-DELLA-MBW-TTG2 module. 2. MBW proteins and TTG2 activate GL2 transcription, while DELLAs repress this activation. 3. gl2-8 seedlings show reduced pectin content, supporting GL2 function in pectin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: gl2-8 reduced pectin but did not show obvious stunted growth or cell-adhesion defect in seedlings under tested conditions
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pGL2:LUC reporter; native GL2 promoter in endogenous context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3 treatment; PAC treatment; light and dark seedling growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray datasets GSE12404; GSE20039 referenced for seed expression context
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TTG2; MYB5; TTG1; EGL3; GAI/RGA/RGLs act upstream on its regulators
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key transcriptional output node; not an enzyme rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for activation of downstream RG-I biosynthetic genes leading to pectin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream regulatory/output step controlling biosynthetic gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate pectin deposition and cell-wall properties, though growth outcomes may depend on tissue context and additional factors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although classified here as a pathway/output gene for extraction purposes, GL2 is a transcription factor rather than a catalytic enzyme; included because it is a core direct node experimentally linked to pectin accumulation in Results

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell adhesion; seedling growth; mucilage production
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: DELLA growth repressor in gibberellin signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of pectin biosynthesis via MBW complex and TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression; competitive inhibition of activator complex assembly and promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Gibberellin (GA) levels; PAC lowers GA and stabilizes DELLAs
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TTG1; MYB5; TT2; EGL3; TT8; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses GL2 activation indirectly by disrupting MBW complex formation and TTG2 function
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interferes with MBW complex and TTG2-MBW activator assemblies
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split-LUC; co-IP; Y3H; dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR under GA3/PAC; genetic analysis with della mutants
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibits pectin biosynthesis; DELLA removal enhances mucilage and seedling pectin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAI physically interacts with MBW components and TTG2. 2. GAI disrupts TTG1-MYB5, TTG1-EGL3, TTG2-MYB5, TTG2-EGL3, and TTG1-TTG2 interactions. 3. GAI suppresses activation of GL2 and thereby represses pectin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major GA-responsive repressor of pectin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3 treatment promotes DELLA degradation; PAC stabilizes DELLA function
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: IP-MS dataset PXD062288 supports DELLA-MYB5 association context
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High DELLA activity correlates with reduced mucilage, reduced seedling pectin, shorter roots/hypocotyls, and cell-adhesion defects
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Negatively correlated with mucilage synthesis genes during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing DELLA repression or tuning GA signaling could enhance pectin/cell-wall traits, but may broadly alter GA-controlled growth
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as representative DELLA in mechanistic assays; dominant DELLA together with RGA in seed mucilage regulation

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: DELLA growth repressor in gibberellin signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB5; TT2; broader MBW/TTG2 module genetically
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses GL2 and downstream pectin genes indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Part of DELLA repression module antagonizing MBW-TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (rga-2; ga1-3 rga-2; rga-24 gai-t6); Y2H; co-IP/IP-MS context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator; loss partially rescues ga1-3 mucilage defect and contributes strongly to elevated mucilage in della combinations
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGA is a dominant DELLA repressing seed mucilage/pectin biosynthesis. 2. rga mutation partially rescues ga1-3 mucilage defect. 3. RGA interacts with MYB5 and TT2 and participates in DELLA repression of the MBW-TTG2 module.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Dominant DELLA component together with GAI
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Repression associated with reduced pectin and growth defects when GA is low
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing dominant DELLA activity could elevate pectin-related wall traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No strong single rga-28 mucilage phenotype, indicating redundancy among DELLAs

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect repression of GL2 and pectin genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Moderate redundant negative regulator of pectin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGL1 interacts broadly with MBW components. 2. Contributes redundantly to DELLA repression of mucilage production. 3. Its effect is evident in higher-order della mutants rather than individually.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stack target with other DELLA genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role inferred from higher-order mutants and interaction data

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect repression of GL2 and pectin genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; co-IP with MYB5 in vivo; higher-order della mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Moderate redundant negative regulator of pectin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGL2 physically associates with MYB5 in vivo and interacts with MBW components. 2. It contributes redundantly to DELLA-mediated repression of mucilage pectin biosynthesis. 3. Higher-order della mutants reveal its contribution to elevated mucilage/RG-I accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stack target with other DELLA loci

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect repression of GL2 and pectin genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Minor or negligible individual contribution, but part of redundant DELLA repression network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RGL3 interacts with MBW components in Y2H. 2. Genetic analysis suggests a minor/negligible contribution compared with RGA/GAI/RGL1/RGL2. 3. Included within the redundant DELLA module repressing pectin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority target relative to RGA/GAI
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contribution mainly inferred from pentuple della context

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell adhesion; seedling growth; mucilage production
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WRKY TF with W-box binding activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of GL2; synergistic activation with MYB5/EGL3; antagonized by DELLAs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GL2, thereby promoting MUM4 and GATL5 expression and pectin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional activating associations with MYB5 and EGL3; interacts with TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GAI; DELLA proteins; TTG1; MYB5; EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; Y2H; split-LUC; co-IP; BiFC; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; epistasis analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pectin biosynthesis; required for GA-induced mucilage production and seedling growth-related pectin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG2 directly binds and activates the GL2 promoter. 2. TTG2 physically interacts with MYB5/EGL3/TTG1 and synergistically activates GL2. 3. DELLAs bind TTG2 and inhibit both its promoter binding and cooperation with MBW proteins. 4. ttg2 mutation abolishes GA-induced mucilage and pectin-related growth responses.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential activator downstream of GA-DELLA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3 increases TTG2 binding to GL2; PAC decreases binding; light/dark seedling contexts
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ttg2 mutants show reduced pectin, stunted growth, impaired GA responsiveness, and cell-adhesion defects
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for modulating pectin deposition and growth-associated wall traits; should consider pleiotropic developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTG2 is epistatic to DELLA in mucilage biosynthesis and many seedling growth traits

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell adhesion; seedling growth; mucilage production
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: WD40 repeat protein; MBW scaffold component
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Assembly/activation of pectin biosynthesis transcriptional module
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Complex assembly with MYB/bHLH proteins to activate GL2; function antagonized by DELLAs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes GL2 and downstream pectin genes as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core MBW complex component; interacts with TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GAI; RGL1; RGL2; RGL3; MYB5; EGL3; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; Y2H; split-LUC; co-IP; Y3H; dual-LUC; epistasis analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for GA-mediated pectin biosynthesis and related seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 is a core MBW component required for seed mucilage pectin biosynthesis. 2. DELLAs interfere with TTG1 interactions with MYB5/EGL3 and with TTG2. 3. ttg1 mutants fail to respond to GA for mucilage induction and display reduced pectin and growth defects.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential MBW scaffold for GA-responsive pectin regulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ttg1 mutants show severe mucilage defects, reduced pectin, stunted growth, and cell-adhesion defects
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for wall-trait engineering but likely pleiotropic due to broad MBW functions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In seedlings, TTG1 may additionally influence DELLA expression, complicating epistasis relationships

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of pectin biosynthesis genes via GL2 module
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Activates GL2 as part of MBW complex and via collaboration with TTG2; repressed by DELLAs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GL2 and downstream MUM4/GATL5 indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with TTG1 and EGL3/TT8; interacts with TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GAI; RGA; RGL1; RGL2; RGL3; TTG1; EGL3; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Complementation with pMYB5:MYB5-GFP; ChIP-qPCR; Y2H; split-LUC; co-IP; dual-LUC; mutant analysis; overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator; overexpression increases mucilage and partially/completely rescues GA-deficient defects depending on background
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB5 binds the GL2 promoter more strongly under GA3 and less under PAC. 2. MYB5 participates in MBW complex-mediated activation of GL2. 3. DELLAs interact with MYB5 and weaken both MBW assembly and TTG2 collaboration. 4. MYB5 overexpression enhances pectin biosynthesis and rescues ga1-3/ga3ox1-3 defects.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important positive activator in the GA-responsive pectin module
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3; PAC; light and dark growth
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: myb5 single mutant has mild seedling phenotype, implying redundancy; overexpression may alter broader TTG-related traits
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to elevate pectin while bypassing part of GA deficiency; likely best used with context-specific expression or gene stacking
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with TT2 in seed mucilage regulation; seedling role may be partially redundant

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of pectin biosynthesis through MBW/TTG2 modules
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Activator-complex component; synergizes with TTG2; antagonized by DELLAs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes GL2 activation with TTG1/MYB5 and with TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RGL1; RGL2; RGL3; TTG1; MYB5; TTG2; GAI functionally interferes with its interactions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split-LUC; co-IP context; BiFC; Y3H; dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator within MBW and TTG2-associated activator module promoting pectin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EGL3 interacts with TTG1 and TTG2 in activator complexes. 2. EGL3 enhances TTG2-mediated activation of GL2. 3. DELLA/GAI disrupts EGL3-containing complexes and represses GL2 activation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supporting positive activator in MBW-TTG2 module
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate stacking target with MYB5/TTG2 for boosting pectin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No standalone genetic mutant analysis presented in Results for metabolite phenotype

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: MBW-mediated transcriptional regulation of pectin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive regulatory component antagonized by DELLAs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes pectin pathway gene activation through MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H and prior functional context discussed in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to positive regulation of seed mucilage pectin biosynthesis as MBW component
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT2 physically interacts with DELLA proteins. 2. TT2 is part of the MBW module targeted by DELLAs. 3. Its role in this study is mechanistic/supportive rather than directly genetically dissected in Results.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less directly validated than MYB5/TTG1/TTG2 in this paper
